task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS main application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the DEM raster file into QGIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'srtm_41_19' is visible in the Layers panel, and a grayscale elevation map is shown on the map canvas.

task_completion.description: Load the 2D trail vector file into QGIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'proposed_trail_2d' is visible in the Layers panel, and a line representing the trail is shown on the map canvas over the DEM.
The 'Drape (set Z value from raster)' dialog window from the Processing Toolbox is open.

task_completion.description: Configure the Drape tool with the correct input and raster layers
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Drape tool dialog, the 'Input layer' is set to 'proposed_trail_2d' and the 'Raster layer' is set to 'srtm_41_19'.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D trail to the specified GeoPackage path
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Drape tool dialog, the 'Draped' output parameter is set to save to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/trail_3d.gpkg', or there is a success log/notification indicating the file was saved to this path, or the resulting 'trail_3d' layer is loaded in the Layers panel.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGIS GUI to perform the task rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the QGIS graphical interface, not a terminal window executing GDAL/OGR commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual QGIS application and its processing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The QGIS Processing Toolbox and the 'Drape' dialog are actively used, with dropdowns and file pickers being interacted with.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GIS processing by creating a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a text editor or script being used to manually create or copy a file to the expected output path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using QGIS to drape a 2D vector line ('proposed_trail_2d.geojson') over a DEM raster ('srtm_41_19.tif') to create a 3D GeoPackage ('trail_3d.gpkg'). The correct tool in QGIS is 'Drape (set Z value from raster)'. The input vector is a 6-point LineString representing the 'Cape Loop Trail'. The DEM covers Western Cape, South Africa. The output must be saved to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/trail_3d.gpkg'.
